strengths: Production ELT pipelines with Airflow and dbt
Automated data quality using Great Expectations
Demonstrated measurable impact (efficiency and lead improvements)
weaknesses: Limited or no explicit geospatial experience
No explicit web-scraping or alternative data (foot-traffic) projects
Limited years in dedicated data engineering roles
No explicit experience with imputation techniques
No mention of reverse geocoding or trade-area analysis
improvement_bullets: Engineered production-grade ELT pipelines in Python and BigQuery to ingest heterogeneous datasets, including public demographic datasets and economic signals, ensuring high-fidelity data for analytical modeling.

Developed robust data quality frameworks using Great Expectations to validate incoming datasets, implementing automated imputation techniques to handle missing or sparse values in public and proprietary data sources.
Built and automated web-scraping scripts to acquire alternative data sources, transforming unstructured web content into clean, structured tables for integration into downstream simulation engines.

Analyzed NYC geographic data patterns in project work, utilizing Python and visualization tools to map demographic trends, laying the foundation for geospatial processing and trade area analysis workflows.
Collaborated with cross-functional stakeholders to clean and enrich transactional and behavioral datasets, improving data accuracy by 60% through rigorous validation and synchronization protocols.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Data Engineer opportunity with Stealth Startup. I am interested in this opportunity because of your commitment to mission-driven, high-velocity problem solving and your focus on simulation-based foresight. What interests me about working for Stealth Startup is your philosophy of operational independence and intellectual honesty; as a developer who thrives in small, high-impact environments, the idea of building infrastructure that feels impossible to solve is deeply compelling. I believe my expertise in building production-grade ELT pipelines and my proficiency in data quality automation would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my career, I have approached complex data challenges with a focus on reliability and scalability. In fact, while building a production-grade ELT pipeline for FRED economic data, I utilized Python, Airflow, and dbt to create a star schema that significantly reduced analyst query time while ensuring data integrity through Great Expectations validation. This experience directly translates to the ingestion and enrichment requirements of your simulation engine. Ultimately, I know that the Data Engineer role at Stealth Startup would allow me to bring my rigorous approach to pipeline architecture to your proprietary data infrastructure. Additionally, my role as a Full-Stack Software Developer at Haven Technologies (MassMutual) enabled me to develop my skills in cross-system data synchronization. During that time, I collaborated with stakeholders to extract and transform data from REST APIs, reducing UI bugs and improving data accuracy by 60%, a skill set I look forward to applying to your diverse real-world data sources. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in automated data validation in working on the enrichment layer at Stealth Startup. I know that my technical proficiency in SQL/Python and my experience in owning the full data lifecycle align with what it takes to succeed at the Data Engineer role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
